evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance and implementing quality checks
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The responsibilities emphasize quality checks, high availability, documentation, and code reviews with no velocity or shipping-speed language present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: designing data models covering both in-game and out-of-game experiences
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: This is a video game studio's analytics function with no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance signals; errors affect game analytics rather than revenue-critical or regulated outcomes.
evidence.data_team_maturity: collaborating with data scientists on their needs, designing data models covering both in-game and out-of-game experiences
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools and practices (dbt, IaC) appear in responsibilities alongside a distinct data scientist function to collaborate with, indicating a growing function with existing infrastructure rather than a from-zero build.
evidence.jd_authorship: monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance and implementing quality checks
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical duties like maintaining data dictionaries, monitoring pipeline availability, and implementing quality checks show someone who has done this job wrote it.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: collaborating with data scientists on their needs, designing data models covering both in-game and out-of-game experiences
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary audience is the data science function and the modeling layer itself rather than a specific commercial, product, or finance team.
evidence.autonomy_level: developing processes to extract, clean, and transform data from various sources for analysis
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role executes scoped technical work (building pipelines, collaborating on data scientist needs) rather than setting independent strategic direction for the function.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD contains no mention of AI tools or AI-consuming infrastructure anywhere in the text.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Quality checks are listed as a core responsibility the candidate implements and owns as part of pipeline monitoring, making this a responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High availability and performance monitoring reflect operational reliability concern about pipeline failures rather than compliance or stakeholder trust framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: collaborating with data scientists on their needs, designing data models covering both in-game and out-of-game experiences
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes designing new data models with maintaining existing pipeline monitoring and documentation, indicating a mid-maturity function scaling rather than starting from zero.
evidence.work_arrangement: The hybrid model requires presence on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays at the office.
evidence.loss_aversion: monitoring pipelines for high availability and performance — reflects operational concern about pipeline stability rather than compliance.
